items.title: Canberra childcare centre closes suddenly, worker sacked

children.content: Parents at Edge Early Learning in Higgins received an email last night advising the regulator had ordered the centre to immediately shut.

items.title: Canberra United FC finally has a buyer. Who are the new owners?

children.content: The new owners of the national capital's A-League Women side have pointed to the launch of a men's team.

image.altText: Football teammates celebrating.

items.title: Hume Highway reopens after truck driver dies in crash

children.content: A man has died after a fiery crash on the Hume Highway this morning, where two trucks collided and left part of the highway closed for hours.

image.altText: A plume of smoke behind a fire truck on a road in the dark

items.title: Former diplomat blames Telstra landline service for repeated Triple Zero failures

children.content: Telstra promised Alastair Gaisford that his wife's healthcare was a priority. But he says 15 landline outages, including some that could have prevented the ability to call Triple Zero in an emergency, have convinced him otherwise.

gallery.altText: A man with short grey hair sits in a living room with a box labelled "Telstra Files" sitting on his lap.

thumbnail.altText: A man with short grey hair sits in a living room with a box labelled "Telstra Files" sitting on his lap.

items.title: ACT minister responds to claim that hospital turned NSW patient away

children.content: ACT Health Minister Rachel Stephen-Smith says a misunderstanding led a NSW patient to believe they had been denied healthcare in Canberra because they lived over the border.

image.altText: A female doctor writing in a note pad, with a stethoscope on the table.

items.title: Driver charged over fiery multi-truck crash near Goulburn

children.content: A 28-year-old semi-trailer driver is charged with negligent driving over an explosive collision that saw six trucks destroyed in a fire at a rest stop near Goulburn.

image.altText: rfs truck with blaze behind it on highway road

items.title: Man denied assisted dying wins partial victory in early test of ACT law

children.content: A tribunal has ruled on a man's bid to overturn his rejection from the ACT's assisted dying scheme in one of the first tests of how people can challenge such decisions.

image.altText: A building sign that reads 'ACT Civil and Administrative Tribunal'.

items.title: Former ACT politician to stand trial on grooming charges

children.content: Former ACT Attorney-General Gordon Ramsay is committed to stand trial for allegedly grooming a teenage boy between 2022 and 2025.

image.altText: Gordon Ramsay walks outside the court in a suit and tie.

items.title: ANU head apologises for failing to protect Jewish staff, students

children.content: The interim vice-chancellor of the Australian National University has apologised for the institution's failure to protect the psychological safety of Jewish students and staff during pro-Palestinian encampments in 2024.

gallery.altText: Tents set up with buildings in the background on the ANU campus.

items.title: Parents go against court orders after children 'traumatised'

children.content: The Family Court granted John full custody of his kids but he returned them to their mother, saying the judge got it wrong.

items.title: Drivers wait months for faults to be repaired on EVs built by new brands

children.content: As an influx of car brands enters the market, dealers ask for a change to consumer laws to fix a grey area that makes them responsible for warranty repairs.

items.title: 'They were not random attacks': The tankers targeted in the Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The commercial tankers struck as they transited the Strait of Hormuz in recent weeks were not random, but part of a "highly, highly targeted" campaign run by Tehran, according to marine traffic watchers.

image.altText: A black, red and white oil tanker on dark ocean water and under a blue sky.

items.title: A gulf has opened up between the Liberals and the zeitgeist

children.content: The mismatch between where the public resides and what the Liberals are expounding is a deeper challenge — a gulf perhaps unable to be bridged, at least in the short term.

image.altText: A man in a dark suit stares glibly toward the camera

items.title: Vaping in decline as young Australians shift to other nicotine products

children.content: Vaping and smoking rates are on the decline, but a shift to other nicotine products could represent an increase in average use.

items.title: Autocue operator 'made more than $100,000' betting on Trump speeches

children.content: US President Donald Trump's longtime technician allegedly made over $142,000 by placing bets on what Donald Trump would say in speeches.

items.title: 'People want something different': Ardern on the rise of fringe politics

children.content: The former New Zealand prime minister reflects on leading through crisis, becoming a mother while running a country and why she believes voters are craving something different.

image.altText: Jacinda Ardern speaks at a podium. Microphones are pointed at her and men in suits are behind her.

items.title: 'Best place to look for life': Atmosphere discovered on Earth-like planet

children.content: The search for life beyond Earth has marked a new discovery — evidence of an atmosphere on an Earth-like planet beyond our Solar System with modelling suggesting it may be a water world.

children.content: Labor insiders expect ongoing debate about artificial intelligence when party faithful gather to hash out a policy platform to guide the next three years.

items.title: Father 'disgusted' over inadequacy of charges after Laos methanol poisoning

items.title: Friday news quiz: The iconic film Sam Neill rejected, a royal reunion and a mystery outbreak

children.content: Our weekly quiz has the film Sam Neill "didn't think was funny", plus the sweetness at the centre of our galaxy.

items.title: This discovery in a Tokyo bookshop made me run away to the mountains

children.content: In a rare bookstore in Japan, I find a manga written by a convicted cannibal. It becomes the subject of a moral dilemma I've been grappling with as a filmmaker.

items.title: How a compulsive list maker ended up making 'extraordinary' observations

children.content: Mike Connor visited the same park for 40 years and wrote down what he saw. He didn't realise he was incidentally chronicling a changing environment and contributing to science.

image.altText: an elderly man holds binoculars up to his face and looks up into trees

items.title: Owning a home in Sydney has become a pipe dream for many, how about other states?

items.title: 'Substantial clean-up effort' needed for shuttered manganese smelter

children.content: Australia's only manganese alloy smelter has closed, leaving the future of the site uncertain and the country more reliant on imported alloys for steel production.

image.altText: A manganese smelter no longer in use, photographed from behind a fence.

items.title: NT child protection bill opponents show 'moral bankruptcy', MLA says

children.content: Fierce debate has surrounded the Northern Territory government's child protection reforms — which will return to parliament for debate next week — with one MLA saying those opposing it have shown "a degree of moral bankruptcy".

items.title: Uber reverses 'disability tax' after outcry

children.content: Uber has reversed its decision to impose a $5 fee on passengers requiring extra assistance, after advocates and service providers labelled it discriminatory and a "disability tax".

image.altText: An uber sign on a car.

items.title: Protesters criticise Zelenskyy after Ukrainian defence minister's sacking

children.content: A dispute between the now former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov and Kyiv's top general is spilling into public, after Volodymyr Zelenskyy orders a second reshuffle of this year.

items.title: Men charged but 15 guns allegedly stolen in Coles Bay robbery not yet recovered

children.content: Three men are charged in separate Launceston suburbs over an alleged violent armed robbery and house fire at Coles Bay, on Tasmania's east coast.

image.altText: Aerial view of a rural residence destroyed by fire.

items.title: Revered surfboard designer and intersex advocate dies in house fire

children.content: Kyra Jonsun is being remembered as an advocate for gender-diverse people and a revered surfboard designer following her death in Wollongong last weekend.

image.altText: A woman holds a surfboard while wearing a wide brimmed hat and black swim suit while looking into the distance.

items.title: Win for MS patients facing drug pricing cliff

children.content: Health Minister Mark Butler has accepted independent advice for a rapid review of MS drugs to ensure they remain available on the PBS.

image.altText: A pile of drugs on a table, all are different shapes and sizes.

items.title: One of leading causes of infant deaths sees 'fantastic' decline in NSW

children.content: New South Wales health authorities have hailed a vaccination program that has nearly halved hospitalisations for a potentially deadly respiratory virus among newborn babies a "fantastic" success, as the winter illness peak fast approaches.

image.altText: A close up of a woman in beige knitwear holding a baby, their faces are out of frame
